items.question: Is there any cost to students for using Global Educational Pathways?
items.answer: No — students and families are never charged. We are compensated through incentive arrangements with the universities and colleges students eventually enrol in, which is standard practice for licensed education agents. Recommendations are based on genuine fit, not which institution offers the largest incentive.

items.question: What is a QEAC, and why does it matter?
items.answer: Qualified Education Agent Counsellor (QEAC) is a professional certification from ICEF Academy's Education Agent Training Course, which tests agents on ethical practice and up-to-date knowledge of international education systems. Our founder, Sabina Yasmin, self-funded and completed this certification (Graduate No. #13556, issued 20 October 2024) to formalise the expertise she'd already built through her own migration and education journey.

items.question: Why does Global Educational Pathways only help with Australia?
items.answer: Focusing on a single destination — where our founder and part of our team actually live and work — means our knowledge of visa rules, university requirements, and day-to-day student life is current and firsthand, not secondhand information about a country none of us has lived in.

items.question: Can you help at any stage, or only from the very beginning?
items.answer: Either. Some students come to us before choosing a course; others already have an offer and need help with documentation or the visa stage. We can step in wherever a student currently is in the process.

items.question: Do you help with postgraduate and research applications too?
items.answer: Yes. As a working professor, our founder has particular insight into what admissions committees look for in Master's, MBA, and PhD applications, including identifying potential supervisors and scholarship opportunities.

items.question: What happens after a student arrives in Australia?
items.answer: Because part of our team is genuinely based in Sydney, we can continue to support students in person after arrival — not just remotely — including practical orientation and staying reachable as a known contact in a new country.

items.question: Who will I actually be talking to?
items.answer: Global Educational Pathways is a small family team: Sabina leads assessment and application strategy, one team member in Bangladesh handles day-to-day messages and communication, and another based in Sydney supports students locally after arrival. You'll always know who you're speaking with.
